User Job: Open the sales-sheet builder with the correct customer and pricing context
Capability: Load inventory with client-sensitive pricing inside the builder
Trigger: Open the builder or change the selected client
Success Criteria: Inventory, client context, and pricing-sensitive rows load together without masking customer-specific price behavior
Permissions / Access Rules: sales workspace access; clients read; inventory read
Validations / Guards: Selected client must resolve; pricing context must refresh safely; unsaved state must remain visible when the data context changes

Capability: Manage selected items, ordering, price overrides, and builder view state

Success Criteria: The working sheet remains editable and dense enough for operators without losing composition control

Flow Guide Sections: none found for dedicated sales-sheet section; Domain 4.1 Orders
Discrepancies / Open Questions: The pack previously under-signaled auto-save, draft deletion, and dirty-state handling
User Job: Save work in progress and reopen it without losing context or converting stale content
Capability: Persist, auto-save, restore, and delete in-progress drafts with trustworthy dirty-state handling
Trigger: Type into the builder, wait for auto-save, save manually, reopen a draft, or delete a draft
Success Criteria: Draft contents restore accurately, auto-save does not fire on first load, and dirty-state gates remain trustworthy before conversion
Permissions / Access Rules: sales workspace access; sales sheet edit
Current UI Surface: SalesSheetCreatorPage draft dialog and auto-save state
Validations / Guards: Auto-save must only run when client, items, and draft name exist; draft lookup must match the active draft; deletion must not leave stale draft state behind
Outputs / Side Effects: Draft state persists, reloads, and can be safely removed when obsolete
Hidden Dependencies: Auto-save timer discipline; currentDraftId continuity; conversion gating
Failure Mode if Lost: Operators lose work, reload the wrong draft, or convert stale content
Parity Test Requirement: Draft save, auto-save, restore, and delete regression
Evidence Required: Draft save proof; auto-save proof; draft reload proof; draft delete proof

Capability: Convert a saved sheet into an order or live-shopping session with explicit unsaved-change gating

Success Criteria: Downstream conversions stay understandable, route to the correct sibling module, and remain blocked until the sheet is safely saved

Validations / Guards: Unsaved changes must block conversion; route handoff must target the created order or live session; downstream context must stay tied to the originating sheet
Outputs / Side Effects: Order creation and live-session creation remain available from Sales Sheets
